facts.text: Cedarhurst got its name from the Cedarhurst Company that bought land there in 1887, a nod to the grove of trees that once stood by the post office.
Cedarhurst was incorporated as a village in 1910, marking its formal establishment on Long Island's South Shore.
Rail service arrived in 1869, sparking growth and leading to the 1878 construction of the Rockaway Hunting Club in Cedarhurst.
The opening scene of the 1987 film Married to the Mob was shot at Cedarhurst's train station, giving the town a spot in Hollywood history.
The Peninsula Public Library, located in Cedarhurst, serves as a community hub for books, events, and local history.
Central Avenue in Cedarhurst buzzes with boutiques, kosher restaurants, cafés, bakeries, and specialty shops, creating a small‑town charm with citylike energy.
